Client Decisions Required

Items the pipeline deliberately did not fabricate. Resolve these before publication.

Claims awaiting verification

  • [MKT-023] On average, a commercial company is looking at about $74,200 in cost for a not-at-fault accident; an accident involving a fatality averages about $500,000; accidents with no injuries and minor damage run roughly $10,000 to $20,000. On Azuga’s driver-scoring platform, a 10-point score increase (e.g., 40 to 50) yields on average about a 57% reduction in the scorecard behaviors. (source attempt: Call Transcript_ Justin.txt, accident-cost / driver-scoring segment (line 73))
  • [MKT-024] The average cost of an accident or a fatality is over $500,000, far exceeding years of investment in the Azuga platform. (source attempt: Call Transcript_ Justin.txt, ROI/accident-cost segment (line 43))
  • [MKT-025] Azuga has roughly a 60% market infiltration in the United States today (per partnerships leadership, experience-based, not independently verified). (source attempt: Call Transcript_ Justin.txt, market-penetration segment (line 61))
  • [MKT-026] Azuga reports approximately $100M in annual revenue with a stated 2026 target of $120M (self-reported, not independently verified). (source attempt: pipeline_metadata.json intake_flags; Call Transcript_ Doug.txt)

Intake ambiguities

  • No customer or partner interviews in the set; all nine transcripts are Azuga executives/employees, so external Voice-of-Customer evidence must come from research, not interviews.
  • Company name appears as ‘Azuga’ throughout; speakers occasionally mispronounce/misspell it (Asuga, Azugo) in transcription, but the subject company is Azuga (azuga.com).
  • Annual revenue self-reported at ~$100M with a stated 2026 target of $120M (Doug); not independently verified.
  • Interview dates not stated in any transcript.
  • Call Transcript_ Tammy.txt: ambiguous speaker_role indeterminate beyond ‘Director’

Quality items not auto-resolved

  • None.
